refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' and 'history of baselines/calibration updates.' High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and operator identity; (c) all updates are logged with before/after values and justification; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline record is complete and auditable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance targets as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, operator, version).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement and calibration updates with full audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure traceability from worksite inputs through derived requirements to baseline records.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Enforce safety margin derivation and validation for all safety-critical performance targets.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters (via Tasking interface), applies deterministic derivation rules and safety margin algorithms, outputs performance targets, and logs all changes to a persistent baseline store with full audit trail.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation with Local Caching – Performance derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, each maintaining local baseline copies; periodic synchronization to a central audit log.
DP-C: Manual Baseline Entry with Post-Hoc Validation – Site Manager manually enters performance targets via UI; system validates against mission context and logs changes; no automatic derivation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters (via Tasking interface), applies deterministic derivation rules and safety margin algorithms, outputs performance targets, and logs all changes to a persistent baseline store with full audit trail.

potential_sources: DP-A depends on Tasking interface to receive mission context; this is a known, well-defined interface.
DP-A outputs performance targets that feed into Vehicle and Teleoperation operational logic; this is a unidirectional dependency (low coupling).
DP-A maintains its own persistent baseline store; no tight coupling to other modules' internal state.
Safety margin derivation is encapsulated within DP-A; changes to safety rules do not ripple across multiple modules.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling because it centralizes derivation logic and baseline management, reducing diffusion of performance requirement changes. The Tasking interface is the only external dependency, and it is already established in the system architecture.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context is reliably provided via Tasking interface with sufficient detail (terrain, payload, cycle count, energy budget, availability SLA).
Assumption: Deterministic derivation rules and safety margin algorithms can be formally specified and validated by Safety Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders.
Risk: If mission context is incomplete or inconsistent, derived performance targets may be invalid; mitigation: implement validation rules and alert mechanisms.
Risk: Safety margin algorithms may require frequent updates as operational experience accumulates; mitigation: design DP-A for easy rule updates without code changes.
Risk: Baseline store may grow large over time; mitigation: implement retention policies and archival strategies.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend adding explicit specification of: (1) derivation rule set (e.g., cycle time = f(terrain, payload, operator skill)); (2) safety margin calculation (e.g., availability target = mission SLA + 5% buffer); (3) baseline versioning and rollback capability; (4) audit log retention policy.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Safety Services module shall own the Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A), including: (1) derivation rule engine for performance targets; (2) safety margin calculation and validation; (3) baseline recording and versioning; (4) audit trail logging; (5) traceability mapping from worksite inputs to derived requirements. Safety Services is the appropriate owner because performance requirement derivation is inherently a safety-critical function (high safety criticality per risk tailoring factors), and Safety Services is the designated module for safety-related logic and compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services receives worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, energy budget, availability SLA, operator skill level) from Tasking interface. This input drives the performance derivation engine.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services outputs derived performance targets and baseline metadata (timestamp, version, source, operator identity) via Telemetry interface to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for operational use and monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services outputs safety margin validation results and alerts (e.g., 'derived availability target below mission SLA') via SafetyAlert interface to Safety Manager and system operators.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system container. Assigning DP-A to NGHE Solution would place performance derivation at the system level, but this is too coarse-grained and would diffuse ownership across multiple sub-modules. Not recommended.

Vehicle module is responsible for vehicle-level control and operation. Performance derivation is a system-level concern that transcends a single vehicle instance. Assigning DP-A to Vehicle would create diffusion if multiple vehicles exist, and would couple Vehicle logic to mission context derivation. Not recommended.

Teleoperation module handles remote operation and tele-operator interface. Performance derivation is not inherently a teleoperation concern; it is a system-level, safety-critical function. Assigning DP-A to Teleoperation would create artificial coupling and diffusion. Not recommended.

Safety Services is the designated module for safety-critical logic, compliance, and safety-related system functions. Performance requirement derivation is inherently safety-critical (high safety criticality per risk tailoring factors). Safety margin calculation and validation are core safety functions. Baseline management and audit trail are compliance and traceability functions. DP-A is highly cohesive with Safety Services' mission. Recommended.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services is the optimal owner of DP-A (Performance Derivation Engine) because: (1) performance requirement derivation is a safety-critical function; (2) safety margin calculation and validation are core safety responsibilities; (3) baseline management and audit trail support compliance and traceability; (4) Safety Services is already responsible for safety-related system logic; (5) centralizing DP-A in Safety Services minimizes diffusion and coupling to other modules.
containment_risks: Risk: Safety Services may become a bottleneck if performance derivation is computationally intensive or frequently invoked; mitigation: implement caching and asynchronous processing.

Risk: Safety Services may lack domain expertise in worksite mission context (terrain, payload, energy budgets); mitigation: involve Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality in rule specification and validation.
Risk: Tight coupling between Safety Services and Tasking interface may create a single point of failure; mitigation: implement fallback mechanisms and graceful degradation if Tasking is unavailable.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign DP-A to Safety Services. Recommend adding explicit interface contracts: (1) Tasking input schema (mission context fields and validation rules); (2) Telemetry output schema (performance targets and metadata); (3) SafetyAlert output schema (validation results and alert types). Also recommend defining Safety Services' internal architecture to separate derivation engine, baseline store, and audit log as distinct sub-components for clarity and testability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR CR-NGHE-005 clearly specifies what the system must do: derive, record, and manage performance requirements from worksite mission context with full traceability and audit trail. Selected DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses all aspects of the refined FR: (1) automatic derivation from mission context via Tasking interface; (2) baseline recording with metadata; (3) managed history with audit trail; (4) traceability from inputs to requirements; (5) safety margin derivation and validation. The FR-DP mapping is sound and exhibits low coupling.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk: DP-A is assigned to a single module (Safety Services), minimizing duplication and fragmentation.
Low diffusion risk: External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) are well-defined and known, reducing ad-hoc coupling.

Residual risk: If Safety Services becomes overloaded with multiple safety-critical functions, performance derivation may be deprioritized; mitigation: monitor Safety Services workload and consider future modularization if needed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services is the optimal container for DP-A with a containment score of 0.95. The module boundary is clear: Safety Services owns derivation logic, baseline store, and audit trail. External interfaces are minimal and well-defined. Ownership is unambiguous. The DP-IM mapping exhibits high cohesion and low coupling.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible within Safety Services: (1) Derivation logic can be unit-tested against known mission contexts and expected performance targets; (2) Baseline recording can be verified by inspecting baseline store records and metadata; (3) Audit trail can be verified by replaying historical updates and confirming traceability; (4) Safety margin validation can be tested against safety requirements and SLAs. All verification activities are contained within Safety Services and do not require cross-module coordination.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to Safety Services is clear and unambiguous. Safety Services is responsible for: (1) implementing derivation rules; (2) managing baseline store; (3) maintaining audit trail; (4) validating safety margins; (5) providing outputs via Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces. No ambiguity or overlap with other modules.

recommended_revisions: Specify derivation rule set formally (e.g., as a decision table or algorithm specification) and include in Safety Services design documentation.
Define baseline versioning and rollback procedures to support operational flexibility and compliance.
Establish audit log retention policy and archival strategy to manage long-term data growth.
Implement validation rules for mission context inputs to detect incomplete or inconsistent data early.
Add explicit error handling and alert mechanisms for derivation failures or safety margin violations.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 Refined is not atomic; it decomposes into five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5). Each sub-FR requires further functional decomposition to specify detailed design parameters and implementation modules. The refined FR is sound and the DP-IM assignment is solid, but the next level of detail is necessary to fully specify the Performance Derivation Engine, baseline management, and audit trail mechanisms. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify implementation details and reduce design risk.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance targets as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, operator, version).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement and calibration updates with full audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure traceability from worksite inputs through derived requirements to baseline records.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Enforce safety margin derivation and validation for all safety-critical performance targets.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is reliably provided via Tasking interface with sufficient detail (terrain, payload, cycle count, energy budget, availability SLA).
Deterministic derivation rules and safety margin algorithms can be formally specified and validated by Safety Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders.
Safety Services has sufficient computational capacity to perform real-time or near-real-time performance derivation.
Baseline store can be implemented as a persistent, auditable data structure within Safety Services.
Traceability from worksite inputs to derived requirements can be maintained through explicit metadata and logging.
open_risks: If mission context is incomplete or inconsistent, derived performance targets may be invalid; mitigation: implement validation rules and alert mechanisms.
Safety margin algorithms may require frequent updates as operational experience accumulates; mitigation: design DP-A for easy rule updates without code changes.
Baseline store may grow large over time; mitigation: implement retention policies and archival strategies.
Safety Services may become a bottleneck if performance derivation is computationally intensive; mitigation: implement caching and asynchronous processing.
Tight coupling between Safety Services and Tasking interface may create a single point of failure; mitigation: implement fallback mechanisms and graceful degradation.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of derivation rule set (e.g., cycle time = f(terrain, payload, operator skill)).
Formal specification of safety margin calculation (e.g., availability target = mission SLA + 5% buffer).
Baseline versioning and rollback procedure documentation.
Audit log retention policy and archival strategy.
Validation rules for mission context inputs.
Error handling and alert mechanisms for derivation failures.
Performance and scalability analysis for baseline store and audit trail.
Traceability matrix mapping worksite inputs to derived requirements to baseline records.
